item.title: 最大化开发者“生产力和效率”的“8个有效技巧” | Top "8 Effective Tips" to Maximize Developer "Productivity and Efficiency"
item.description:
在我们职业生涯中的某个时刻，我们都在努力解决一些事情，但要富有成效。 作为一名开发人员，当我们开始为公司或客户工作时，我们大多数人都会沉迷于编码。 我们花了很多时间在电脑屏幕前编写代码、修复错误、阅读技术资料以及尝试编程中的新事物。 我们喜欢这样做，甚至在周末我们也会花费无尽的时间进行编码。 我们中的许多人也开始认为，离开电脑仅仅意味着我们很懒，而且我们工作不努力。

作为开发人员，最大限度地提高生产力对于高效处理项目和交付高质量代码至关重要。 为了帮助您提高工作效率，这里有八个有效的技巧，可以对您的工作流程产生重大影响：

为了保持专注和生产力，请尽量减少工作环境中的干扰。 关闭通知、将手机静音，并创建一个专门的工作空间，让您可以不受干扰地集中精力。 正如比尔·盖茨曾经说过的那样： “集中注意力并充分利用时间的能力就是一切。” 通过消除干扰，您可以将全部注意力集中在编码和解决问题上。

避免随机任务切换并致力于结构化工作流程。 确定任务的优先级并按顺序完成它们，而不是在不同的任务之间跳转。 正如史蒂夫·乔布斯的名言： “决定不做什么与决定做什么同样重要。” 这种方法有助于保持注意力，提高效率，并减少与持续上下文切换相关的精神负担。

虽然多任务处理似乎是一种完成更多任务的方法，但它通常会导致生产力下降和工作质量下降。 相反，一次专注于一项任务，完成它，然后继续下一项任务。 研究表明，同时处理多项任务会使工作效率降低高达 40%。 通过全神贯注于每项任务，您可以在更短的时间内取得更好的结果。

投入时间了解集成开发环境 (IDE) 的细节。 熟悉它的功能、快捷方式和插件，可以简化您的编码过程。 正如 Joel Spolsky 所说， “最有效的调试工具仍然是仔细的思考，加上明智地放置打印语句。” 一个利用良好的 IDE 可以节省您宝贵的时间，并使您的编码体验更加高效。

熟练使用命令行执行运行测试、版本控制和部署应用程序等任务。 与图形界面相比，命令行工具通常提供更大的灵活性和速度。 正如 Linus Torvalds 曾经说过的那样： “空谈是廉价的。给我看看代码。” 通过掌握命令行，您可以快速高效地完成开发任务。

识别工作流程中的重复任务并使用脚本或工具将其自动化。 自动化代码格式化、测试和部署等任务可以显着提高您的工作效率。 正如亚伯拉罕·林肯所说： “给我六个小时砍一棵树，我会用前四个小时磨斧头。” 通过减少手动工作，您可以专注于更高级别的问题解决和代码开发。

通过结对编程和代码审查与其他开发人员协作可以提高代码质量、更早发现错误并增强学习能力。 参与建设性讨论、分享知识和接收反馈可以带来更好的解决方案并加速您作为开发人员的成长。 正如Andrew Hunt 和 David Thomas在《务实的程序员》中所写， “结对工作的程序员编写的代码平均效率提高了 15%。”

不断扩展您的技能对于长期生产力至关重要。 投入时间学习符合您的目标和兴趣的新技术、框架或编程语言。 此外，从事不同的项目可以帮助您获得宝贵的经验并克服挑战，为您作为开发人员的整体发展做出贡献。 正如约翰·伍登（John Wooden）所说， “重要的是你在了解了一切之后所学到的东西。”

通过实施这八个有效技巧，您可以提高开发人员的工作效率并优化您的工作流程。 请记住，生产力不仅意味着更努力地工作，还意味着更聪明地工作。 找到最适合您的策略并将其融入您的日常生活中。 通过持续的实践和改进，您将成为一名更高效、更成功的开发人员。 正如吉姆·罗恩（Jim Rohn）明智地说的那样： “要么你掌控这一天，要么那一天掌控你。” 掌控您的工作效率，让它推动您在编码之旅中取得更大成就。



原文:

Being productive at something we all struggle with at some point in our career. As a developer when we start working for a company or a client, most of us get obsessed with coding. We spend a lot of time in front of the computer screen writing the code, fixing the bug, reading about the technical stuff, and trying new things in programming. We enjoy doing it and we spend endless hours in coding even on weekends. A lot of us also start thinking that moving away from our computer simply means we are lazy and we aren’t a hard worker.

As a developer, maximizing productivity is crucial for efficiently tackling projects and delivering high-quality code. To help you boost your productivity, here are eight effective tips that can make a significant difference in your workflow:

1. Minimize Distractions


To maintain focus and productivity, minimize distractions in your work environment. Turn off notifications, silence your phone, and create a dedicated workspace where you can concentrate without interruptions. As Bill Gates once said, "The ability to concentrate and to use your time well is everything." By eliminating distractions, you can devote your full attention to coding and problem-solving.

2. Be Less Random

Avoid random task switching and aim for a structured workflow. Prioritize your tasks and work on them sequentially rather than jumping between different assignments. As Steve Jobs famously said, "Deciding what not to do is as important as deciding what to do." This approach helps maintain focus, improves efficiency, and reduces the mental overhead associated with constant context switching.

3. Don’t Multitask


While multitasking may seem like a way to accomplish more, it often leads to decreased productivity and lower quality work. Instead, focus on one task at a time, complete it, and then move on to the next. Research has shown that multitasking can reduce productivity by up to 40%. By giving each task your full attention, you can produce better results in less time.

4. Master Your IDE

Invest time in learning the ins and outs of your Integrated Development Environment (IDE). Familiarize yourself with its features, shortcuts, and plugins that can streamline your coding process. As Joel Spolsky said, "The most effective debugging tool is still careful thought, coupled with judiciously placed print statements." A well-utilized IDE can save you valuable time and make your coding experience more efficient.

5. Work On the Command Line


Become proficient in using the command line for tasks such as running tests, version control, and deploying applications. Command line tools often offer more flexibility and speed compared to graphical interfaces. As Linus Torvalds once stated, "Talk is cheap. Show me the code." By mastering the command line, you can navigate your development tasks swiftly and efficiently.

6. Automate


Identify repetitive tasks in your workflow and automate them using scripts or tools. Automating tasks like code formatting, testing, and deployment can significantly enhance your productivity. As Abraham Lincoln said, "Give me six hours to chop down a tree, and I will spend the first four sharpening the axe." By reducing manual effort, you can focus on higher-level problem-solving and code development.

7. Invest Time in Pair Programming and Code Review


Collaboration with other developers through pair programming and code review can improve code quality, catch bugs earlier, and enhance learning. Engaging in constructive discussions, sharing knowledge, and receiving feedback can lead to better solutions and accelerate your growth as a developer. As Andrew Hunt and David Thomas wrote in "The Pragmatic Programmer," "Programmers who work in pairs produce code that is 15% more productive on average."

8. Learn New Skills and Work on New Projects


Continuously expanding your skill set is essential for long-term productivity. Invest time in learning new technologies, frameworks, or programming languages that align with your goals and interests. Additionally, working on diverse projects can help you gain valuable experience and overcome challenges, contributing to your overall development as a developer. As John Wooden said, "It's what you learn after you know it all that counts."

By implementing these eight effective tips, you can enhance your productivity as a developer and optimize your workflow. Remember, productivity is not just about working harder but also working smarter. Find the strategies that work best for you and incorporate them into your daily routine. With consistent practice and improvement, you'll become a more efficient and successful developer. As Jim Rohn wisely stated, "Either you run the day, or the day runs you." Take control of your productivity and let it propel you towards greater achievements in your coding journey.
item.author: soumyadeepdey

item.title: AINIRO Oracle，废话搜索引擎 | AINIRO Oracle, the Cut the Crap Search Engine
item.description:
我今天和昨天花了几个小时创建了AINIRO Oracle，Cut The Crap 搜索引擎。 我的动机？ 当我寻找东西时，我厌倦了在成吨的垃圾中跋涉。

例如，想要弄清楚《1984》这本书出版于哪一年，需要通读西班牙内战的整个章节、乔治·奥威尔的出生、他 47 次婚姻中的 11 次、它们如何结束，以及他的妻子有谁是他们的兄弟姐妹 - 那时浏览他的名气等等。找到这些简单的事实通常需要 30 分钟。 使用我们的搜索引擎，只需 30 秒即可找到答案。

  • 基本上，与维基百科相比，提高了 5 个数量级。

    为了大声呼喊，我正在寻找一个简单的数字，这个数字是1949！

    当然，出于显而易见的原因，系统中的第一个问题必须是 42 。

    这不是搜索引擎

    警告，这不是搜索引擎，而是“问答机” 。 我们还添加了一些技巧来使这一点变得清晰。 你必须用?来结束你的问题。 如果您不这样做，系统将拒绝回答您。 这是为了将我们与其他搜索引擎区分开来，在其他搜索引擎中，您通常会用神秘的准英语来表达问题，例如“site:stackoverflow.com cohesion encapsulation polymorphism” 。

    这是自然语言答录机，而不是搜索引擎！

    争论杀手机器

    我怀疑它的另一个非常有用的功能是，每次 Facebook 上的一些笨蛋向你询问来源时，它都会作为“来源提供者” 。 我们甚至在所有文章的底部明确添加了来源，以明确这一点，允许您向我们的预言机表达您的问题，复制 URL，然后将其粘贴到一些愚蠢的 Facebook 辩论中，其中一些笨蛋会向您索要来源，然后继续你的生活......

    它在内部使用 DuckDuckGo 和 ChatGPT，您将获得 DuckDuckGo 搜索结果的链接，并且文章将被缓存。 如果您提出的问题是其他人之前问过的，或者您的问题“相似度”达到 90% 或以上，并且之前提供的答案距离不到 5 天 - 机器将返回之前的答案。 这是为了减少令牌计数并节省数据库空间。

    嘘，请随意在您希望的任何地方分享它提出的任何答案。 在辩论等中“重复使用”答案是我们创建此内容的很大一部分动机。

一旦被暂停，polterguy 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，polterguy 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，polterguy 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 polterguy 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 polterguy：

使 polterguy 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

坚持不懈的 polterguy 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

I spent a couple of hours today and yesterday and created AINIRO Oracle, the Cut The Crap Search Engine. My motivation? I'm tired of wading through tons of garbage when I search for things.

For instance, trying to figure out what year the book 1984 was published requires reading through entire sections of the Spanish Civil War, George Orwell's birth, 11 of his 47 marriages, how they ended, and who his wifes had as their siblings - For then to skim through his claim to fame, etc. Finding simple facts like these typically takes 30 minutes. With our search engine, finding the answer takes 30 seconds.

  • When Was The Book 1984 Published?

Basically, 5 orders of magnitudes improvement compared to WikiPedia.

For crying out loud, I was looking for a simple number, the number was 1949!

Of course, the very first question in the system had to be what is 42 for obvious reasons.

This is NOT a Search Engine

Warning, this is not a search engine, it's a "question/answer machine". We've added a little bit of finesse to make that clear too. You have to finish your question with a ?. If you don't, the system will refuse to answer you. This is to separate us from other search engines where you typically phrase questions in cryptic quasi English, such as "site:stackoverflow.com cohesion encapsulation polymorphism".

This is a natural language answer machine and not a search engine!

Argument killer machine

Another one of its features I suspect will be highly useful, is as a "source provider" every time some schmuck at Facebook asks you for a source. We've even explicitly added sources at the bottom of all articles to make this clear, allowing you to phrase your question to our oracle machine, copy the URL, and paste it into some stupid Facebook debate where some schmuck asks you for a source, for then to simply move on with your life ...

Internally it's using DuckDuckGo and ChatGPT, and you will get a link to DuckDuckGo's search results, and the article will be cached. If you ask a question somebody else asked previously, or your question has a "similarity" of 90% or more, and the answer previously provided is less than 5 days old - The machine will return the previous answer. This is to reduce the token count, and conserve database space.

Psst, feel free to share whatever answers it comes up with anywhere you wish. "Re-using" answers in debates and such, was a big part of our motivation to create this.

item.title: 另一篇时事通讯哈哈：1000 只恐龙 | Yet Another Newsletter LOL: 1000 Dinosaurs
item.description:
又一周，另一份时事通讯。 让我们开始吧！

网络周围

好玩的东西

我的国家的人们让我们感到自豪，哈哈。

本周智慧名言

如果你想取得不同的结果，有时你必须愿意改变你当前的行为。 我们不能总是期望通过做我们一直在做的同样的事情来得到不同的结果。

无耻的插头

我们讨论了制作、物联网消息传递 (MQTT)、MicroPython 和 Mastodon。 再次感谢吊死安迪！

记得点赞和订阅哦！ 另外，本周在nickyt.live上与Graham Ritchie一起玩的感觉非常棒。 我们讨论了可访问性，提供了一些可以利用的技巧和工具，甚至还进行了小型审核。

这里重点展示了 Chrome DevTools 对比检查器的运行情况。 再次感谢您的悬挂，格雷厄姆！

最后，我将参加（不是发言）下周在多伦多举行的 Refactor DX 2023 。 如果您参加，请不要害羞，过来打个招呼！

工作

我和其他人一样，在 iamdeveloper.com 社区中发布职位信息以及所有其他类型的内容。 如果您正在寻找另一个友好的互联网角落，请前往discord.iamdeveloper.com 。

原文:

Another week, another newsletter. Let's get to it!

Around the Web

Fun Stuff

Dance party for 1,000 dinosaurs follows Dundurn, Sask., challenge to world record. Folks in my country making us proud lol.

Words of Wisdom for the Week

If you want to achieve different results, you sometimes must be willing to make changes to your current actions. We can't always expect to get different results by doing the same things we've always done.

– Addy Osmani on Threads

Shameless Plugs

The stream recording with Andy Piper is up on YouTube. We discussed making, IoT messaging (MQTT), MicroPython and Mastodon. Thanks again for hanging Andy!

Remember to like and subscribe! 😎

Also, it was awesome hanging with Graham Ritchie this week on nickyt.live. We discussed accessibility, provided some tips and tools to leverage, and even squeezed in a mini-audit.

Here's a highlight showing the Chrome DevTools contrast checker in action. Thanks again for hanging, Graham!

Lastly, I'm attending (not speaking) Refactor DX 2023 in Toronto this coming week. If you're attending, don't be shy and come say hi!

Jobs

I post jobs in the iamdeveloper.com community, plus all other kinds of content, as do others. If you're looking for another friendly nook of the internet, head to discord.iamdeveloper.com.

item.title: 为什么你应该开始为开源做出贡献 | Why You should Start Contributing to OpenSource
item.description:
夜鸟07

发表于 7 月 9 日

我的开源项目之旅始于去年夏天，当时我决定投身比特币项目。 然而，我很快意识到浏览大量可用资源是相当困难的。 在本文中，我将分享一些克服这一挑战的简单方法，并提供有关为什么您应该开始为开源项目做出贡献的见解。

首先，需要注意的是，并非所有开源项目都需要常规贡献者的贡献。 有些项目是由核心团队开发和维护的，而另一些项目则积极寻求社区的贡献。 在深入项目之前，有必要了解项目的目标、开发流程以及它们是否鼓励社区贡献。

为开源项目做出贡献的一个有趣的方面是有机会重构其他人编写的代码。 虽然我们经常听说重构自己的代码的重要性，但探索和改进别人的代码也同样有趣。 它使我们能够分析不同的编码风格、思维过程和解决问题的方法。 开源项目提供了接触才华横溢的开发人员编写的代码并学习他们的专业知识的机会。

当您深入开源世界时，您会遇到各种各样的项目。 首先，通过识别与您的兴趣和技能相符的特定开源项目来缩小搜索范围会很有帮助。 发现开源项目的最佳平台之一是 GitHub，它托管着大量的公共代码存储库。 您可以浏览不同的项目，探索他们的问题和讨论，并寻找贡献的机会。

如果您不确定从哪里开始， Up For Grabs等网站可能是宝贵的资源。 他们整理了积极寻求贡献的开源项目列表，使您可以更轻松地找到适合您的技能和兴趣的项目。 这些平台为您的研究提供了一个起点，并帮助您驾驭广阔的开源项目空间。

现在，您正在等待检查AGE并拉取一些请求。

原文:

NightBird07

Posted on Jul 9

 

My journey in the world of open source projects began last summer when I decided to dive into the Bitcoin project. However, I quickly realized that navigating through the vast amount of resources available was quite overwhelming. In this article, I will share some simple ways to overcome this challenge and provide insights on why you should start contributing to open source projects.

Firstly, it's important to note that not all open source projects require contributions from regular contributors. Some projects are developed and maintained by a core team, while others actively seek contributions from the community. Before diving into a project, it's essential to understand the project's goals, development process, and whether they encourage community contributions.

One interesting aspect of contributing to open source projects is the opportunity to refactor code written by others. While we often hear about the importance of refactoring our own code, exploring and improving someone else's code can be equally intriguing. It allows us to analyze different coding styles, thought processes, and approaches to problem-solving. Open source projects offer a chance to engage with code written by talented developers and learn from their expertise.

When you dive into the world of open source, you'll encounter a diverse range of projects. To get started, it's helpful to narrow down your search by identifying specific open source projects that align with your interests and skills. One of the best platforms to discover open source projects is GitHub, which hosts a vast amount of public code repositories. You can browse through different projects, explore their issues and discussions, and find opportunities to contribute.

If you're unsure where to begin, websites like Up For Grabs can be valuable resources. They curate lists of open source projects that actively seek contributions, making it easier for you to find projects that are suitable for your skills and interests. These platforms provide a starting point for your research and help you navigate the vast space of open source projects.

now, what you are waiting for check the AGE and pull some requests.

item.title: 全球化网站：国际化和本地化的力量🚀🔥 | Globalising Websites: The Power of Internationalisation and Localisation 🚀🔥
item.description:
在当今的互联世界中，万维网对于希望接触更广泛的受众并打开新市场的企业和组织至关重要。 本文探讨了国际和区域企业在网络开发中的重要性，强调了它们帮助企业在全球范围内蓬勃发展的力量。

了解国际化

国际化是设计和构建网站以使其能够轻松适应不同语言、文化和地区的过程。 企业可以通过采用国际惯例获得许多好处。 首先，网站的国际化扩大了他们的影响范围，使他们能够接触到全球受众并与来自不同文化的用户建立联系。
这种扩张为增长和创收开辟了新的机会。

此外，国际化还改善了国际用户的用户体验。 通过以他们自己的语言提供内容并考虑他们的文化期望，网站可以创造积极的体验并与各个领域的用户建立信任。 这种个性化体验可以提高客户参与度和交易价值。

在 Web 开发中使用国际化

为了在国际上取得成功，Web 开发人员需要考虑很多事情。
全球内容包括语言支持和翻译服务，可轻松本地化文本和媒体内容。 此外，全球用户界面 (UI) 工作的重点是创建多语言 UI 设计并改变不同语言和阅读风格的方向。

解决创建文件和处理数据存储以适应多种语言和字符的常见后端问题。 此外，服务器端本地化技术可帮助网站根据用户的语言偏好和位置更新内容。

本地化的作用

本地化在使网站适应当地市场方面发挥着重要作用。
它包括定制内容、UI 元素和整体用户体验，以适应目标地区或国家的文化、偏好和理解。 本地化不仅仅是翻译，因为它涉及改变图像、颜色、符号和其他元素以引起当地受众的共鸣。

企业可以在该地区投资，为用户创造有意义且有吸引力的体验。 适合文化并反映区域利益的网站更有可能与当地客户建立联系并建立强大的品牌。

本地化技术考虑因素

本地化需要注重细节，以实现不同语言和地区之间的用户交流。
它还包括管理不同语言的 UI 布局，因为文本长度和方向可能不同。 除了

检查结果和更改对于准确性和本地知识非常重要。 需要全面的现场测试和质量保证程序来识别和解决现场工作期间可能出现的问题。

国际化和本地化工具和技术

许多工具和技术可用于促进国际化和本地化过程。 内容管理系统 (CMS) 提供有助于管理多种语言内容的功能和插件。 翻译管理系统 (TMS) 为有效的翻译管理提供了基础。 国际化和本地化库以及框架（例如 gettext 和 ICU）提供了编程工具和资源来帮助构建国际网站。
区域一体化平台允许企业与翻译人员和当地居民合作，从而实现更好的沟通和区域效率。

全球成功的最佳实践

为了成功地将网站国际化，企业必须遵循最佳实践才能取得成功。 良好的市场研究对于了解目标受众、他们的偏好和文化差异至关重要。 制定良好的区域战略有助于企业有效地协调其努力和资源。

与翻译人员和当地人合作，提供高质量和文化翻译。 使用使用上述工具和技术的本地计算机可以简化流程并节省时间和资源。
定期监控和收集反馈使企业能够根据客户流量和不断变化的业务条件审查和改进其本地网站。

结论

在数字连接时代，通过国际和区域手段进行网络全球化对于旨在扩大影响力并与全球受众建立联系的企业来说是一项强大的战略。 通过接受来自不同语言和文化的用户，企业可以创造个性化且引人入胜的体验，建立信任并开辟新的增长机会。 网络全球化不仅仅是一种趋势，更是一种趋势。 它已成为企业在当今全球经济中取得成功的必要条件。



原文:

In today's connected world, the World Wide Web is essential for businesses and organisations looking to reach a wider audience and open new markets. This article examines the importance of international and regional businesses in web development, highlighting their power to help businesses thrive worldwide.

Understanding Internationalisation

Internationalisation is the process of designing and building a website so that it can be easily adapted to different languages, cultures and regions. Businesses can derive many benefits by using international practices. First, internationalising the website expands their reach, allowing them to reach a global audience and connect with users from different cultures.
This expansion opens up new opportunities for growth and revenue generation.

In addition, internationalisation improves the user experience for international users. By providing content in their own language and taking into account their cultural expectations, websites can create a positive experience and build trust with users in a variety of fields. This personalised experience leads to customer engagement and transactional value.

Using Internationalisation in Web Development

To be successful internationally, Web developers need to consider many things.
Global content includes language support and translation services to enable easy localisation of text and media content. Additionally, worldwide user interface (UI) work focuses on creating multilingual UI design and changing the orientation for different languages ​​and reading styles.

Solves common back-end problems with creating files and handling data storage to accommodate multiple languages ​​and characters. Additionally, server-side localisation techniques help websites update content based on user's language preferences and location.

The Role of Localisation

Localisation plays an important role in adapting a website to a local market.
It includes customising content, UI elements, and overall user experience to fit the culture, preferences, and understanding of the target region or country. Localisation is more than translation, as it involves changing images, colors, symbols and other elements to resonate with the local audience.

Businesses can invest in the region to create a meaningful and engaging experience for users. Websites that are culturally appropriate and reflect regional interests are more likely to connect with local customers and build a strong brand.

Localisation Technical Considerations

Localisation requires attention to detail to enable user communication between languages ​​and regions.
Localisation of UI elements includes adapting UI text and icons to local languages ​​while maintaining installation integrity. It also includes managing the UI layout for different languages, as text length and direction can be different. In addition to the

UI content, websites must specify the date, time and release number created to meet regional conventions. Checking results and changes is important for accuracy and local knowledge. Comprehensive field testing and quality assurance procedures are required to identify and resolve issues that may arise during fieldwork.

Internationalisation and Localisation Tools and Techniques

Many tools and techniques are available to facilitate the internationalisation and localisation process. Content management systems (CMS) provide features and plug-ins that help manage content in multiple languages. A translation management system (TMS) provides the basis for effective translation management. Internationalisation and localisation libraries and frameworks such as gettext and ICU provide programming tools and resources to help build international websites.
The regional integration platform allows businesses to collaborate with translators and local residents, resulting in better communication and regional efficiency.

Best Practices for Global Success

To successfully internationalise their websites, businesses must follow best practices to achieve success. Good market research is essential to understanding your target audience, their preferences and cultural differences. Having a good regional strategy helps businesses coordinate their efforts and resources effectively.

Work with translators and local people to provide quality and cultural translations. Using a local machine using the above tools and techniques can simplify the process and save time and resources.
Regular monitoring and collecting feedback allows businesses to review and improve their local websites based on customer traffic and changing business conditions.

Conclusion

In the age of digital connections, web globalisation through international and regional means is a powerful strategy for businesses aiming to expand their reach and connect with a global audience. By accepting users from different languages ​​and cultures, businesses can create personalised and engaging experiences, build trust and open new opportunities for growth. Web globalisation isn't just a trend, it's a trend. It has become a necessity for businesses to be successful in today's global economy.
item.author: arjuncodess

item.title: 您能解释一下 package.json 文件中“^1.2.3”的含义吗？ | Can you explain the meaning of '^1.2.3' in a package.json file?
item.description:
阿什瓦尼3011

发表于 7 月 9 日

在这篇文章中，让我们一起深入探讨 npm 版本控制并揭示它的重要性！

示例：~1.2.3 包括 1.2.4、1.2.9，但不包括 1.3.0。

示例：^1.2.3 包括 1.2.4、1.3.0，但不包括 2.0.0。

快乐编码！

原文:

ashwani3011

Posted on Jul 9

 

In this post, let's dive into npm versioning together and uncover its significance!

🌟 npm Versioning Basics:

1️⃣ Major Version (X.y.z): Signifies significant changes, often incompatible with previous versions.

2️⃣ Minor Version (x.Y.z): Introduces new features while maintaining backward compatibility.

3️⃣ Patch Version (x.y.Z): Reserved for bug fixes and minor updates, with no new features.

🌟 Symbols:

1️⃣ Tilde (~):

~ allows patch updates and compatible bug fixes.
Example: ~1.2.3 includes 1.2.4, 1.2.9, but not 1.3.0.

2️⃣ Caret (^):

^ includes minor and patch updates, including new features.
Example: ^1.2.3 includes 1.2.4, 1.3.0, but not 2.0.0.

🤔 So, how can we use this knowledge in our projects?

1️⃣ If you're developing an open-source library:

🌟 Increment the major version for breaking changes and major updates.

🌟 Increment the minor version for introducing new features and enhancements while maintaining backward compatibility.

🌟 Increment the patch version for bug fixes and minor updates that don't introduce new features.

2️⃣ When using someone else's library.

🌟 Use the ~ (tilde) symbol to allow patch updates and compatible bug fixes.

🌟 Use the ^ (caret) symbol to allow minor and patch updates, including new features.

🌟 Specify an exact version (without symbol) when you want to be precise about the dependency version used.

Happy coding!

item.title: 为什么选择PGSQL作为资源存储的数据库 | Why Choose PGSQL as the Database for Resource Storage
item.description:
介绍

在当今的云计算环境中，对策略即代码（PaC）工具的需求不断增加，以实现多云安全、成本分析和组织结构分析等任务。 本文将探讨为什么选择 PGSQL 作为资源存储的数据库，并重点介绍其对于此类查询分析 PaC 工具的优势。

可扩展性

在云环境中，资源的数量和规模会随着时间的推移而增长。 数据一致性

作为关系数据库，PGSQL 提供强大的事务支持，确保数据一致性。 强大的查询能力

通过利用PGSQL强大的查询功能，Selefra可以轻松地对多云环境中的资源进行深入分析，为用户提供有价值的见解。

例如：

 SELECT DISTINCT(a1.*) FROM aws_s3_buckets a1, aws_s3_bucket_grants a2 WHERE a1.selefra_id = a2.aws_s3_buckets_selefra_id AND a2.grantee :: jsonb ->> 'URI' = 'http://acs.amazonaws.com/groups/global/AllUsers' AND a2.permission IN ('WRITE_ACP', 'FULL_CONTROL');

安全

在 PaC 工具中，安全性是一个至关重要的问题，尤其是在处理敏感数据时。 通过使用 PGSQL 作为资源存储的数据库，Selefra 可以利用这些安全功能来保护用户数据，确保未经授权的各方无法访问敏感信息。

成熟的生态系统

这意味着可以随时获得有关性能调整、安全最佳实践和错误修复的支持和建议。 结论

选择PGSQL作为Selefra资源存储的数据库是一个明智的决定。 通过利用PGSQL的优势，Selefra可以提供高效、可靠、安全的云服务资源分析，为用户提供更好的多云管理体验。

推荐阅读

How to spot and fix issues with publicly accessible AWS S3 buckets 如何发现和修复可公开访问的 AWS S3 存储桶的问题

How Selefra Combines GPT for Multi-Cloud Security Scanning Selefra 如何结合 GPT 进行多云安全扫描



原文:

Introduction

In today's cloud computing environment, the demand for Policy-as-Code (PaC) tools is increasing to achieve tasks such as multi-cloud security, cost analysis, and organizational structure analysis. Selefra is an excellent PaC tool that uses YAML to read policies and relies on PGSQL database for storing cloud service resources. This article will explore why PGSQL is chosen as the database for resource storage and highlight its advantages for such query analysis PaC tools.

Scalability

PGSQL is a mature open-source Relational Database Management System (RDBMS) with excellent scalability. In the cloud environment, the number and scale of resources can grow over time. PGSQL's scalability allows it to handle large volumes of data and supports horizontal and vertical scaling, meeting the storage requirements of Selefra in different-scale environments.

Data Consistency

As a relational database, PGSQL provides robust transaction support, ensuring data consistency. Selefra needs to read and analyze data from cloud service resources and perform policy checks based on it. PGSQL's transaction mechanism guarantees the integrity and consistency of data across multiple queries and analysis operations, enhancing the tool's reliability and accuracy.

Powerful Query Capabilities

Selefra requires flexible and efficient querying and analysis of cloud service resources. PGSQL offers rich query capabilities, supporting complex SQL queries, aggregation operations, and window functions, among others. By leveraging PGSQL's powerful querying capabilities, Selefra can easily perform in-depth analysis of resources in multi-cloud environments, providing valuable insights to users.

Such as:

SELECT DISTINCT(a1.*)
FROM aws_s3_buckets a1, aws_s3_bucket_grants a2
WHERE a1.selefra_id = a2.aws_s3_buckets_selefra_id AND a2.grantee :: jsonb ->> 'URI' = 'http://acs.amazonaws.com/groups/global/AllUsers' AND a2.permission IN ('WRITE_ACP', 'FULL_CONTROL');

Security

In PaC tools, security is a crucial concern, especially when dealing with sensitive data. PGSQL provides multi-level security features, including role-based access control, SSL/TLS encryption, and transparent encryption storage of data. By using PGSQL as the database for resource storage, Selefra can leverage these security features to protect user data, ensuring that sensitive information remains inaccessible to unauthorized parties.

Mature Ecosystem

PGSQL, as a popular database management system, has a large and active community and ecosystem. This means that support and advice on performance tuning, security best practices, and bug fixes are readily available. Selefra can benefit from the knowledge and resources of the PGSQL community, staying up-to-date with the latest developments in database technology and obtaining support when needed.

Conclusion

Choosing PGSQL as the database for resource storage in Selefra is a wise decision. PGSQL's scalability, data consistency, powerful query capabilities, security features, and mature ecosystem make it an ideal choice for query analysis-based Policy-as-Code tools. By harnessing the advantages of PGSQL, Selefra can provide efficient, reliable, and secure analysis of cloud service resources, offering users a better experience in multi-cloud management.

GitHub: github.com/selefra/selefra

Slack: selefra.io/community/join

recommended reading

How to spot and fix issues with publicly accessible AWS S3 buckets

How Selefra Combines GPT for Multi-Cloud Security Scanning
item.author: bineshsamal

item.title: 如何使用 React 跟踪按下状态下的鼠标和触摸移动 | How To Track Mouse and Touch Move In Pressed State With React
item.description:
当我们制作复杂的交互式 UI（如滑块、编辑器或颜色选择器）时，我们经常需要在按下状态下跟踪鼠标或触摸移动。 让我分享一个专门为此目的而设计的易于使用的抽象组件。

该组件接收两个属性：

  • 它接收一个具有props和position属性的对象。 在我所有的实际案例中，我需要知道相对位置，所以我决定使用它作为默认值。
  • 它接收一个具有相同position参数的对象。 请注意，当压力原点位于容器外部时，位置将为null 。
 import { Point } from "lib/entities/Point" import { useBoundingBox } from "lib/shared/hooks/useBoundingBox" import { enforceRange } from "lib/shared/utils/enforceRange" import { MouseEvent, MouseEventHandler, ReactNode, TouchEvent, TouchEventHandler, useCallback, useEffect, useState, } from "react" import { useEvent } from "react-use" interface ContainerProps { onMouseDown: MouseEventHandler<HTMLElement> onTouchStart: TouchEventHandler<HTMLElement> ref: (node: HTMLElement | null) => void } interface ChangeParams { position: Point | null } interface RenderParams extends ChangeParams { props: ContainerProps } interface PressTrackerProps { render: (props: RenderParams) => ReactNode onChange?: (params: ChangeParams) => void } export const PressTracker = ({ render, onChange }: PressTrackerProps) => { const [container, setContainer] = useState<HTMLElement | null>(null) const box = useBoundingBox(container) const [position, setPosition] = useState<Point | null>(null) const handleMove = useCallback( ({ x, y }: Point) => { if (!box) return const { left, top, width, height } = box setPosition({ x: enforceRange((x - left) / width, 0, 1), y: enforceRange((y - top) / height, 0, 1), }) }, [box] ) const handleMouse = useCallback( (event: MouseEvent) => { handleMove({ x: event.clientX, y: event.clientY }) }, [handleMove] ) const handleTouch = useCallback( (event: TouchEvent) => { const touch = event.touches[0] if (touch) { handleMove({ x: touch.clientX, y: touch.clientY }) } }, [handleMove] ) useEffect(() => { if (onChange) { onChange({ position }) } }, [onChange, position]) const clearPosition = useCallback(() => { setPosition(null) }, []) useEvent("mouseup", position ? clearPosition : undefined) useEvent("touchend", position ? clearPosition : undefined) useEvent("mousemove", position ? handleMouse : undefined) useEvent("touchmove", position ? handleTouch : undefined) return ( <> {render({ props: { ref: setContainer, onMouseDown: handleMouse, onTouchStart: handleTouch, }, position: position, })} </> ) }

要启动跟踪过程，使用者组件应呈现PressTracker组件并将必要的 props 传递给容器元素。

 <PressTracker render={({ props, position }) => ( <Container {...props}> {position && ( <Highlight style={{ width: toPercents(position.x), height: toPercents(position.y), }} /> )} </Container> )} />

我们在触摸开始事件上设置鼠标按下的位置。 因此，一旦用户与容器交互，我们就开始react-use库中的useEvent钩子跟踪光标位置。 它监听mouseup和touchend事件以停止跟踪，以及mousemove和touchmove事件以更新光标位置。 特哈斯·马哈詹 - 七月 20 '21

一旦被暂停，rodionchachura 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，rodionchachura 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，rodionchachura 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 rodionchachura 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 rodionchachura：

使 rodionchachura 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

坚持不懈的 rodionchachura 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

✨ Watch on YouTube | 🐙 GitHub | 🎮 Demo

When we make complex interactive UIs like sliders, editor or color pickers, we often need to track mouse or touch movement in a pressed state. Let me share an easy-to-use abstract component designed precisely for this purpose.

PressTracker component

The component receives two properties:

  • render - a function that consumer component should use to render the content. It receives an object with props and position properties. The props field contains ref and mouse and touch start handlers, it should be passed to the container element. The position is a point with x and y coordinates in the range from 0 to 1. It represents the relative position of the cursor inside the container. In all my practical cases, I needed to know the relative position, so I decided to use it as a default.
  • onChange - a function that will be called when the position changes. It receives an object with the same position argument. Note that when the press origin is outside the container, the position will be null. 
import { Point } from "lib/entities/Point"
import { useBoundingBox } from "lib/shared/hooks/useBoundingBox"
import { enforceRange } from "lib/shared/utils/enforceRange"
import { MouseEvent, MouseEventHandler, ReactNode, TouchEvent, TouchEventHandler, useCallback, useEffect, useState,
} from "react"
import { useEvent } from "react-use" interface ContainerProps { onMouseDown: MouseEventHandler<HTMLElement> onTouchStart: TouchEventHandler<HTMLElement> ref: (node: HTMLElement | null) => void
} interface ChangeParams { position: Point | null
} interface RenderParams extends ChangeParams { props: ContainerProps
} interface PressTrackerProps { render: (props: RenderParams) => ReactNode onChange?: (params: ChangeParams) => void
} export const PressTracker = ({ render, onChange }: PressTrackerProps) => { const [container, setContainer] = useState<HTMLElement | null>(null) const box = useBoundingBox(container) const [position, setPosition] = useState<Point | null>(null) const handleMove = useCallback( ({ x, y }: Point) => { if (!box) return const { left, top, width, height } = box setPosition({ x: enforceRange((x - left) / width, 0, 1), y: enforceRange((y - top) / height, 0, 1), }) }, [box] ) const handleMouse = useCallback( (event: MouseEvent) => { handleMove({ x: event.clientX, y: event.clientY }) }, [handleMove] ) const handleTouch = useCallback( (event: TouchEvent) => { const touch = event.touches[0] if (touch) { handleMove({ x: touch.clientX, y: touch.clientY }) } }, [handleMove] ) useEffect(() => { if (onChange) { onChange({ position }) } }, [onChange, position]) const clearPosition = useCallback(() => { setPosition(null) }, []) useEvent("mouseup", position ? clearPosition : undefined) useEvent("touchend", position ? clearPosition : undefined) useEvent("mousemove", position ? handleMouse : undefined) useEvent("touchmove", position ? handleTouch : undefined) return ( <> {render({ props: { ref: setContainer, onMouseDown: handleMouse, onTouchStart: handleTouch, }, position: position, })} </> )
}

The PressTracker component stores the container element in the useState and utilizes the useBoundingBox hook to obtain its position and size. To initiate the tracking process, the consumer component should render the PressTracker component and pass the necessary props to the container element.

<PressTracker render={({ props, position }) => ( <Container {...props}> {position && ( <Highlight style={{ width: toPercents(position.x), height: toPercents(position.y), }} /> )} </Container> )}
/>

We set the position on either mouse down on touch start events. So once the user have interacted with the container, we start tracking the cursor position with the useEvent hook from the react-use library. It listens for mouseup and touchend events to stop tracking, as well as mousemove and touchmove events to update the cursor position. The handleMove function converts absolute coordinates from touch and mouse events, ensuring that the coordinates remain within the range of 0 to 1, even when the cursor is outside the container.

Tejas Mahajan - Jul 20 '21

item.title: 深入了解 Laravel Sanctum 代币功能 | Dive into Laravel Sanctum Token Abilities
item.description:
亚西尔·埃尔加马尔

发表于 7 月 9 日

此功能称为 Sanctum Token Abilities

通过使用 Sanctum 令牌能力，您可以使用 Laravel sainttum 能力，您可以在创建令牌后指定能力，

您可以使用此功能来管理由于特定能力而产生的角色和路线，

例如，您可以使用 Sanctum Token Abilities 来设置只有具有特定能力的用户才能访问的路线。 您还可以根据用户的能力执行操作，例如显示或隐藏某些功能或数据。

它很简单，如果您没有多个或复杂的角色，您可以使用它

我们通过例子来练习一下：

在此示例中，我们保护 /admin/index 路由，以便只有具有 [moderator & admin] 能力的用户才能访问它。

 Route::middleware(['auth:sanctum','abilities:moderator,admin'])->prefix('admin')->group(function () { Route::get('index', [AdminController::class, 'index']); });

在此示例中，我们保护 /admin/index 路由，以便只有具有 [主持人或管理员] 能力的用户才能访问它，这意味着用户至少有一个可以访问该路由的权限。

 Route::middleware(['auth:sanctum','ability:moderator,admin'])->prefix('admin')->group(function () { Route::get('index', [AdminController::class, 'index']); });

此外，我们可以检查用户令牌是否具有特定的能力，例如通过我们的控制器，

 if (auth()->user()->tokenCan('admin')) { dd('Hello Admin'); }

概括：
这些功能可用于管理角色并限制对应用程序某些部分的访问。

特雷西| 软件工程师 - 6 月 2 日

一旦被暂停，yasserelgammal 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，yasserelgammal 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，yasserelgammal 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 yasserelgammal 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 yasserelgammal：

使 yasserelgammal 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 yasserelgammal 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Yasser Elgammal

Posted on Jul 9

Laravel has great feature including in Sanctum, that allow to identify logged user and handle the authorize process through token,

This feature is called Sanctum Token Abilities

By using Sanctum Token Abilities you can With Laravel sanctum abilities you can specify ability once you create token,

You can use this ability to manage roles and routes due to specific abilities,

For example, you can use Sanctum Token Abilities to set routes that are accessible only to users with specific abilities. You can also perform actions based on a user's abilities, such as showing or hiding certain features or data.

It's simple and you can use it if you don't have multiple or complex roles

Let's practise with example:

1- Add following Middlewares lines to $middlewareAliases inside App\Http\Kernel

'abilities' => \Laravel\Sanctum\Http\Middleware\CheckAbilities::class,
'ability' => \Laravel\Sanctum\Http\Middleware\CheckForAnyAbility::class,

2- Assign ability to user with poweful sanctum token by usign this:

$user->createToken('token-name', ['admin'])->plainTextToken;

3- Now we can protect our routes, by specifying abilites

In this example, we're protecting the /admin/index route so that only users with the [moderator & admin] ability can access it.

Route::middleware(['auth:sanctum','abilities:moderator,admin'])->prefix('admin')->group(function () { Route::get('index', [AdminController::class, 'index']); });

In this example, we're protecting the /admin/index route so that only users with the [moderator or admin] ability can access it, it means or user has at least one with access the route.

Route::middleware(['auth:sanctum','ability:moderator,admin'])->prefix('admin')->group(function () { Route::get('index', [AdminController::class, 'index']); });

Also, we can check if user token has a specific ability for example through our controller,

 if (auth()->user()->tokenCan('admin')) { dd('Hello Admin'); }

Summary:
Sanctum Token Abilities is a feature of the Laravel Sanctum package that allows you to specify abilities for a token when it's created. These abilities can be used to manage roles and restrict access to certain parts of your Application.

Tracy | Software Engineer - Jun 2

item.title: 在 AWS 上构建安全的企业机器学习平台 | Build a Secure Enterprise Machine Learning Platform on AWS
item.description:
埃斯拉·艾哈迈德

发表于 7 月 9 日

本文帮助云工程师、安全工程师、机器学习运维 (MLOps) 工程师和数据科学家了解构建安全的企业机器学习 (ML) 平台的各个组件。 来自 AWS 白皮书文档。

介绍

为金融服务等受监管行业构建企业机器学习平台可能是一项复杂的架构、运营和治理挑战。 架构设计需要考虑很多因素，包括 ML 平台实施中的 AWS 账户设计、网络架构、安全性、自动化管道、数据管理和模型服务架构。
该资源提供架构模式、代码示例和最佳实践，以指导在 AWS 上构建企业机器学习平台。

构建企业机器学习平台需要不同跨职能团队的协作，例如：

  • 云和安全工程师：云工程和安全工程团队负责创建、配置和管理 AWS 账户以及账户中的资源。 他们为不同的业务线和操作环境设置 AWS 账户，并配置网络和安全性。 还可以与其他安全功能（例如身份和访问管理）配合使用，设置所需的用户、角色和策略，以授予用户和服务权限以在 AWS 账户中执行各种操作。

  • 数据工程师：与数据科学家和机器学习工程师密切合作，帮助识别数据源，构建数据管理功能和数据处理管道。 他们围绕数据建立安全控制，以实现数据科学实验和自动化管道。

  • 他们利用不同的服务，例如管道工具、代码存储库、容器存储库、库包管理、模型管理和机器学习培训。

  • 数据科学家和机器学习工程师：他们是平台的最终用户。 他们使用该平台进行实验，例如探索性数据分析、数据准备和特征工程、模型训练和模型验证。

  • 身份和访问管理

    为了建立安全的机器学习环境，需要定义和创建人和机器身份，以允许对环境进行预期访问。 用户角色
    数据科学家/ML 工程角色：IAM 角色提供对主要用于实验的资源和服务的访问。 这些服务可能包括 SageMaker Studio 或 Notebook、用于数据访问的 Amazon S3 以及用于数据查询的 Athena。
    数据工程角色：IAM 角色提供对主要用于数据管理、数据管道开发和运营的资源和服务的访问。 这些服务可能包括 S3、AWS Glue、Amazon EMR、Athena、Amazon RDS 和 SageMaker Notebook。
    这些服务可能包括 SageMaker、AWS CodePipeline、AWS CodeBuild、AWS CloudFormation、Amazon ECR、AWS Lambda 和 AWS Step Functions。

    服务角色
    此角色提供对 S3 存储桶等资源的访问权限，以用于作业的输入和输出。
    同样，SageMaker 笔记本执行角色可用于运行训练作业。
    其他服务角色：其他服务（例如 AWS Glue、Step Functions 和 CodePipeline）也需要在运行作业或管道时承担服务角色。

    下图显示了 SageMaker 用户和 SageMaker 服务功能的用户和服务角色。

克莱门特·马西哈西 - 7 月 6 日

原文:

Esraa Ahmed

Posted on Jul 9

This article helps cloud engineers, security engineers, Machine Learning Ops (MLOps) engineers, and data scientists understand the various components of building a secure enterprise machine learning (ML) platform. from AWS whitepaper documentation.

Introduction

Building an enterprise ML platform for regulated industries such as financial services can be a complex architectural, operational, and governance challenge. There are many architecture design considerations, including AWS account design, networking architecture, security, automation pipelines, data management, and model serving architecture in an ML platform implementation.
AWS offers a comprehensive suite of services that enable the development of flexible, secure, and scalable ML platforms for demanding use cases. This resource provides architecture patterns, code samples, and best practices to guide the construction of an enterprise ML platform on AWS.

Building an enterprise machine learning platform requires the collaboration of different cross-functional teams such as:

  • Cloud and security engineers: Cloud engineering, and security engineering teams are responsible for creating, configuring, and managing the AWS accounts, and the resources in the accounts. They set up AWS accounts for the different lines of business and operating environments and configure networking and security. Also work with other security functions, such as identity and access management, to set up the required users, roles, and policies to grant users and services permissions to perform various operations in the AWS accounts.

  • Data engineers: Work closely with data scientists and ML engineers to help identify data sources, and build out data management capabilities, and data processing pipelines. They establish security controls around data to enable both data science experimentation and automated pipelines.

  • MLOps engineers: Build and manage automation pipelines to operationalize the ML platform and ML pipelines for fully/partially automated CI/CD pipelines, such as pipelines for building Docker images, model training, and model deployment. They utilize different services such as pipeline tools, code repository, container repository, library package management, model management, and ML training.

  • Data scientists and ML engineers: they are the end-users of the platform. They use the platform for experimentation, such as exploratory data analysis, data preparation and feature engineering, model training, and model validation.

  • IT Auditors: Responsible for analyzing system access activities, identifying anomalies and violations, preparing audit reports for audit findings, and recommending remediations.

-_ Model risk managers_: Responsible for ensuring machine learning models meet various external and internal control requirements.

Identity and Access Management

To establish a secure ML environment, both human and machine identities need to be defined and created to allow for the intended access into the environment. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) is the primary access control mechanism you’ll use for provisioning access to AWS resources in your environment.

User Roles
Data scientist/ML engineering role: The IAM role provides access to the resources and services that are mainly used for experimentation. These services could include SageMaker Studio or Notebook, Amazon S3 for data access, and Athena for data querying.
Data engineering role: The IAM role provides access to the resources and services mainly used for data management, data pipeline development, and operations. These services could include S3, AWS Glue, Amazon EMR, Athena, Amazon RDS, and SageMaker Notebook.
MLOps engineering role: The IAM role provides access to the resources and services mainly used for building automation pipelines and infrastructure monitoring. These services could include SageMaker, AWS CodePipeline, AWS CodeBuild, AWS CloudFormation, Amazon ECR, AWS Lambda, and AWS Step Functions.

Service roles
SageMaker notebook execution role: This role is assumed by a SageMaker Notebook instance or SageMaker Studio Application when code or AWS commands (such as CLI) are run in the notebook instance or Studio environment.
SageMaker processing job role: This role is assumed by SageMaker processing when a processing job is run. This role provides access to resources such as an S3 bucket to use for input and output for the job.
SageMaker training/tuning job role: This role is assumed by the SageMaker training/tuning job when the job is run. Similarly, the SageMaker notebook execution-role can be used to run the training job.
SageMaker model execution role: This role is assumed by the inference container hosting the model when deployed to a SageMaker endpoint or used by the SageMaker Batch Transform job.
Other service roles: Other services such as AWS Glue, Step Functions, and CodePipeline also need service roles to assume when running a job or a pipeline.

The figure below shows the user and service roles for a SageMaker user and SageMaker service functions.

Clément Marcilhacy - Jul 6

item.title: 构建明天的网络：SSR 与 CSR - 您应该选择哪条路径？ | Building the Web of Tomorrow: SSR vs. CSR - Which Path Should You Choose?
item.description:
伊山·巴奇

发表于 7 月 9 日

在 Web 开发领域，渲染在提供无缝和交互式用户体验方面发挥着至关重要的作用。 服务器端渲染 (SSR) 和客户端渲染 (CSR) 这两种著名的渲染方法具有明显的优势和注意事项。 这篇博文探讨了 SSR 和 CSR 之间的差异，阐明了它们的优势和挑战，以帮助开发人员在选择最佳渲染策略时做出明智的决策。

了解服务器端渲染 (SSR)

服务器端渲染涉及在将网页发送到客户端浏览器之前在服务器上生成和渲染网页。 在SSR中，服务器根据客户端的请求动态生成HTML，包括内容和结构。 客户端收到一个完全渲染的页面，准备显示。

服务器端渲染的好处

  1. 改进的初始页面加载时间：服务器端渲染可以加快初始页面加载时间，因为服务器将预渲染的页面发送到客户端。 这会带来响应更快、更具吸引力的用户体验，尤其是在较慢的网络连接上。

  2. 这增强了网站的搜索引擎优化（SEO）能力，因为搜索引擎可以更好地理解内容并对其进行排名。

  3. 优雅降级：SSR 确保即使 JavaScript 在客户端浏览器上被禁用或不可用时也可以访问内容。 这种可访问性对于使用辅助技术和搜索引擎爬虫的用户尤其重要。

了解客户端渲染 (CSR)

另一方面，客户端渲染将渲染过程转移到客户端浏览器。 在 CSR 中，服务器向客户端发送一个最小的 HTML 框架，然后用数据填充该框架并由在客户端运行的 JavaScript 进行呈现。

客户端渲染的好处

  1. 增强的交互性和用户体验：客户端渲染可实现高度交互和动态的用户体验。 灵活性和敏捷性：通过 CSR，开发人员可以更灵活地更新和修改页面的特定组件，而无需完全重新加载。 这种敏捷性有助于加快开发周期并实现无缝更新，从而减少对频繁服务器请求的需求。

挑战和考虑因素

  1. 初始加载时间和 SEO 挑战：虽然 CSR 提供​​了增强的交互性，但它通常需要下载和执行更大的 JavaScript 包，从而导致初始页面加载时间较慢。 此外，搜索引擎爬虫在索引客户端渲染生成的动态内容时可能面临挑战，从而影响 SEO。

  2. 复杂性增加：服务器端渲染需要在服务器上生成 HTML，可能会给开发过程带来额外的复杂性。 实施 SSR 通常需要服务器端框架和库的专业知识和考虑因素。

  3. 平衡工作负载：通过服务器端渲染，服务器承担渲染页面的责任，这可能会影响服务器的性能和可扩展性。 仔细的负载平衡和服务器资源管理对于保持最佳性能至关重要。

电子商务网站：

然后，服务器将此预渲染的 HTML 发送到客户端的浏览器，提供快速且 SEO 友好的体验。
当用户选择不同的产品选项或将商品添加到购物车时，JavaScript 会更新页面的特定组件，而无需完全重新加载。 这提供了更具交互性和响应性的体验，但初始页面加载可能会稍微慢一些。

新闻文章网站：

然后，服务器将预渲染的 HTML 发送到客户端的浏览器。 这种方法可确保快速的初始加载时间和文章的搜索引擎可见性。
然后，JavaScript 使用文章文本、图像和相关内容动态填充页面。 这种方法可以使文章之间的过渡更加顺畅，并提供更具互动性的阅读体验。 但是，初始页面可能需要更长的时间才能完全交互。

社交媒体动态：

预渲染的 HTML 被发送到客户端，确保快速的初始加载时间。 搜索引擎还可以有效地对内容进行索引，从而提高可发现性。
然后，JavaScript 动态呈现提要，从而无需重新加载整个页面即可进行实时更新。 这可以实现更具交互性和无缝的用户体验，但可能会导致初始页面加载速度变慢。

结论

这些示例演示了如何在不同场景中应用服务器端渲染（SSR）和客户端渲染（CSR）以实现特定目标。 服务器端渲染擅长提供快速的初始加载时间、SEO 优势和优雅的降级。 另一方面，客户端渲染提供了增强的交互性、灵活性和更新敏捷性。 通过理解这些示例并考虑其项目的要求，开发人员可以选择适当的渲染方法来创建令人愉快且高效的 Web 体验。

原文:

Ishan Bagchi

Posted on Jul 9

 

In the realm of web development, rendering plays a crucial role in delivering seamless and interactive user experiences. Two prominent rendering approaches, server-side rendering (SSR) and client-side rendering (CSR), offer distinct advantages and considerations. This blog post explores the differences between SSR and CSR, shedding light on their strengths and challenges, to help developers make informed decisions when choosing the optimal rendering strategy.

Understanding Server-Side Rendering (SSR)

Server-side rendering involves generating and rendering web pages on the server before sending them to the client's browser. In SSR, the server dynamically generates the HTML, including the content and structure, based on the client's request. The client receives a fully rendered page, ready for display.

Benefits of Server-Side Rendering

  1. Improved Initial Page Load Time: Server-side rendering enables faster initial page load times since the server sends a pre-rendered page to the client. This results in a more responsive and engaging user experience, particularly on slower network connections.

  2. SEO Friendliness: Search engine crawlers can easily index the pre-rendered HTML content delivered by server-side rendering. This enhances the website's search engine optimization (SEO) capabilities, as search engines can better understand and rank the content.

  3. Graceful Degradation: SSR ensures that content is accessible even when JavaScript is disabled or unavailable on the client's browser. This accessibility is particularly important for users with assistive technologies and search engine crawlers.

Understanding Client-Side Rendering (CSR)

Client-side rendering, on the other hand, shifts the rendering process to the client's browser. In CSR, the server sends a minimal HTML skeleton to the client, which is then populated with data and rendered by JavaScript running on the client's side.

Benefits of Client-Side Rendering

  1. Enhanced Interactivity and User Experience: Client-side rendering allows for highly interactive and dynamic user experiences. JavaScript frameworks, such as React or Vue.js, excel at efficiently updating specific parts of a page without reloading the entire page, resulting in a smoother and more fluid user experience.

  2. Flexibility and Agility: With CSR, developers have more flexibility to update and modify specific components of a page without requiring a full reload. This agility facilitates faster development cycles and enables seamless updates, reducing the need for frequent server requests.

Challenges and Considerations

  1. Initial Load Time and SEO Challenges: While CSR offers enhanced interactivity, it typically requires downloading and executing larger JavaScript bundles, resulting in slower initial page load times. Additionally, search engine crawlers may face challenges in indexing dynamic content generated by client-side rendering, impacting SEO.

  2. Increased Complexity: Server-side rendering, with its need to generate HTML on the server, can introduce additional complexity to the development process. Implementing SSR often requires specialized knowledge and considerations for server-side frameworks and libraries.

  3. Balancing Workloads: With server-side rendering, the server bears the responsibility of rendering pages, which can potentially impact server performance and scalability. Careful load balancing and server resource management are crucial to maintain optimal performance.

Examples of SSR and CSR:

E-commerce Website:

SSR: When a user visits a product page on an e-commerce website built with SSR, the server generates the complete HTML for that page, including product details, pricing, and images. The server then sends this pre-rendered HTML to the client's browser, providing a fast and SEO-friendly experience.
CSR: In a CSR approach, the server sends a minimal HTML skeleton to the client, and the page is populated and rendered using JavaScript. When a user selects different product options or adds items to their cart, JavaScript updates specific components of the page without requiring a full reload. This provides a more interactive and responsive experience, but the initial page load may be slightly slower.

News Article Website:

SSR: In an SSR implementation, the server generates the complete HTML for a news article, including the headline, text, images, and related content. The server then sends the pre-rendered HTML to the client's browser. This approach ensures fast initial load times and search engine visibility for articles.
CSR: With CSR, the server sends a minimal HTML structure to the client, and JavaScript fetches the article content from an API. JavaScript then dynamically populates the page with the article text, images, and related content. This approach allows for smoother transitions between articles and provides a more interactive reading experience. However, it may take longer for the initial page to become fully interactive.

Social Media Feed:

SSR: In an SSR-based social media feed, the server generates HTML for the user's feed, including posts, comments, and media. The pre-rendered HTML is sent to the client, ensuring a fast initial load time. Search engines can also index the content effectively, improving discoverability.
CSR: In a CSR approach, the server sends a basic HTML structure to the client, and JavaScript fetches the feed data asynchronously from the server or an API. The JavaScript then dynamically renders the feed, allowing for real-time updates without reloading the entire page. This enables a more interactive and seamless user experience but may result in a slower initial page load.

Conclusion

These examples demonstrate how server-side rendering (SSR) and client-side rendering (CSR) can be applied in different scenarios to achieve specific goals. Server-side rendering excels in providing fast initial load times, SEO advantages, and graceful degradation. On the other hand, client-side rendering offers enhanced interactivity, flexibility, and agility in updates. By understanding these examples and considering the requirements of their projects, developers can choose the appropriate rendering approach to create delightful and efficient web experiences.

item.title: 添加旋转和缩放🕹️🌟到我的游戏引擎 | Adding Rotation and Scaling🕹️🌟 to my Game Engine
item.description:
开发者们大家好！ 我带着一些消息回来了！ 我为我们的游戏对象添加了旋转和图像缩放。 有很多方法可以做到这一点，这就是我所做的。

在我之前的文章中，我尝试在我的引擎Helicity.ai中制作一个 flappy 小鸟游戏，很明显，我们需要旋转和缩放来获得正常且可用的体验。

我发现这一系列事件特别有趣，因为*这就像拿起一支铅笔并将其移动到我们想要绘制某些内容的位置，旋转页面，然后绘制，然后旋转回页面并将铅笔带回到原来的位置。 这是Github

游戏对象渲染方式的更改

看一下 GameObject 类。 清除画布后，我们每帧都会调用“渲染”方法。

 // GameObject.js render() { if (this.image.loaded) { Renderer.drawImage( this.image, this.x, this.y, this.width * this.xscale, this.height * this.yscale, this.angle, this.alpha ); } else { //No image was provided. Just drawing a black block. Renderer.drawBlock( "black", this.x, this.y, this.width * this.xscale, this.height * this.yscale, this.angle, this.alpha ); } }

游戏对象现在根据“xscale”和“yscale”属性进行缩放，并根据“angle”属性进行旋转。 您可以完全控制这些值🎮。

但这两个函数中会发生什么？ 让我们来看看

现在，让我们进入 Renderer 类：

 // Renderer.js drawImage(image, x, y, width, height, angle, alpha) { this.context.save(); this.context.translate(x, y); this.context.rotate(-angle); this.context.globalAlpha = alpha; this.context.drawImage(image, 0, 0, width, height); this.context.restore(); } drawBlock(color, x, y, width, height, angle, alpha) { this.context.save(); this.context.translate(x, y); this.context.rotate(-angle); this.context.globalAlpha = alpha; this.context.fillStyle = color; this.context.fillRect(0, 0, width, height); this.context.restore(); }

事件顺序的解释

注意到一些新的东西了吗？ 让我们分解一下渲染器的drawImage 和drawBlock 方法中发生了什么。 它们都遵循相似的操作顺序。

这允许我们应用变换，然后在完成绘图后恢复到原始状态。

  1. 平移原点(context.translate(x, y))：translate(x, y) 函数将画布的 (0,0) 原点移动到提供的 (x,y) 点。 这特别有用，因为画布中的旋转始终围绕当前原点执行。 因此，通过将原点平移到我们想要旋转的点（在本例中为图像或块的中心），我们准备画布以进行旋转。

  2. 旋转画布（context.rotate(-angle)）：现在原点位于图像或块的中心，我们可以旋转画布。 我们使用 -angle 是因为画布的正旋转是顺时针方向，而通常在 2D 游戏中，我们将正旋转视为逆时针方向。

  3. 设置透明度(context.globalAlpha = alpha)：globalAlpha 属性用于将透明度应用于我们的图像或块。 它可以采用 0.0（完全透明）和 1.0（完全不透明）之间的值。

  4. 绘制图像或块：通过设置转换，我们现在可以绘制图像或块。 对于图像，我们使用drawImage(image, 0, 0, width, height)方法，它将指定的图像绘制到画布上。 对于块，我们在使用 fillStyle = color 设置填充颜色后使用 fillRect(0, 0, width, height) 方法。

  5. 恢复画布状态（context.restore()）：完成绘制后，我们使用restore()方法将画布恢复到原始状态。 这将从堆栈中弹出最近保存的状态并应用它。 我们应用的所有变换（平移、旋转和 globalAlpha 更改）都将被撤消，因此它们不会影响画布上的任何进一步绘制。

这一系列操作使我们能够将图像和块绘制到画布上，并控制它们的位置、大小、旋转和透明度，从而为对象在画布上的显示方式提供了很大的灵活性。

结论

因为我们这样做了，所以我们现在可以让游戏中的小鸟旋转，使其看起来像是在坠落。
您可以尝试这个游戏的变体。

 //In the update method of the bird //change angle of bird to make it look like it is falling. -90 is straight down //dont make it indefinitely dependent on velocityY, or else it will keep rotating this.angle = Math.min(this.velocityY * 0.05, 90);

然而，也存在一些新的挑战。

物理

这个可爱的功能不足以处理旋转哈哈。

 //Adjusted for scaling but NOT rotation static checkCollision(object1, object2) { return ( object1.x < object2.x + object2.width * object2.xscale && object1.x + object1.width * object1.xscale > object2.x && object1.y < object2.y + object2.height * object2.yscale && object1.y + object1.height * object1.yscale > object2.y ); }

精灵起源

因此，到目前为止，游戏对象的原点，其 x 或 y 是图像的左上角。 现在这很好，但这意味着所有旋转都会围绕该点发生，使得这只鸟看起来像是在以一种有点奇怪的方式移动......但它是一只鸟，所以它看起来很正常，哈哈。

如果您喜欢这个并且对开发感兴趣，请考虑加入不和谐服务器！

原文:

Hello, developers! 👋💖

I'm back with some news! I added rotation and image scaling to our game objects. There are many approaches to doing this and this is what I have done.

In my previous post I tried making a flappy bird game in my engine Helicity.ai and it was clear that we needed Rotation and Scaling for a normal and usable Experience.

I find this sequence of events particularly interesting because *it is like taking a pencil and moving it to where we want to draw something , rotating the page and then drawing and then rotating back the page and bringing the pencil back to where it was. *

Here is the Github

Changes to how a Game Object is Rendered

Take a peek at the GameObject class. We're calling the 'render' method every frame after clearing the canvas.

// GameObject.js
render() {
  if (this.image.loaded) {
    Renderer.drawImage(
      this.image,
      this.x,
      this.y,
      this.width * this.xscale,
      this.height * this.yscale,
      this.angle,
      this.alpha
    );
  } else {
//No image was provided. Just drawing a black block.
    Renderer.drawBlock(
      "black",
      this.x,
      this.y,
      this.width * this.xscale,
      this.height * this.yscale,
      this.angle,
      this.alpha
    );
  }
}

The GameObject now scales according to 'xscale' and 'yscale' properties and rotates according to the 'angle' property. You have full control over these values 🎮.

But what happens in both those functions? Let's see

Now, let's journey to our Renderer class: 

// Renderer.js
drawImage(image, x, y, width, height, angle, alpha) {
  this.context.save();
  this.context.translate(x, y);
  this.context.rotate(-angle);
  this.context.globalAlpha = alpha;
  this.context.drawImage(image, 0, 0, width, height);
  this.context.restore();
}

drawBlock(color, x, y, width, height, angle, alpha) {
  this.context.save();
  this.context.translate(x, y);
  this.context.rotate(-angle);
  this.context.globalAlpha = alpha;
  this.context.fillStyle = color;
  this.context.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);
  this.context.restore();
}

Explanation of the Sequence of Events

Notice something new? The 'drawImage' and 'drawBlock' methods now include 'angle' and 'alpha' parameters.

Let's break down what's happening in the Renderer's drawImage and drawBlock methods. They both follow a similar sequence of operations.

1.** Saving the Canvas State** (context.save()): Before any transformations, we save the current state of the canvas. The save() method pushes the current state onto a stack. This allows us to apply transformations and then revert back to the original state after we've completed our drawing.

  1. Translating the Origin (context.translate(x, y)): The translate(x, y) function shifts the (0,0) origin of our canvas to the provided (x,y) point. This is particularly useful because rotation in the canvas is always performed around the current origin. So by translating the origin to the point around which we want to rotate (in this case, the center of our image or block), we prepare the canvas for rotation.

  2. Rotating the Canvas (context.rotate(-angle)): Now that the origin is at the center of our image or block, we can rotate our canvas. The rotate(angle) function rotates the canvas around the current origin by the specified angle (measured in radians). We use -angle because the canvas's positive rotation is clockwise, while typically in 2D games, we treat positive rotation as counterclockwise.

  3. Setting Transparency (context.globalAlpha = alpha): The globalAlpha property is used to apply transparency to our image or block. It can take a value between 0.0 (fully transparent) and 1.0 (fully opaque).

  4. Drawing the Image or Block: With our transformations set, we can now draw the image or block. For images, we use the drawImage(image, 0, 0, width, height) method, which draws the specified image onto the canvas. For blocks, we use the fillRect(0, 0, width, height) method after setting the fill color with fillStyle = color.

  5. Restoring the Canvas State (context.restore()): After we're done with drawing, we restore the canvas back to its original state by using the restore() method. This pops the most recently saved state from the stack and applies it. All of the transformations we applied (translation, rotation, and globalAlpha change) are undone, so they won't affect any further drawing on the canvas.

This sequence of operations lets us draw images and blocks onto the canvas with control over their position, size, rotation, and transparency, giving a lot of flexibility in how objects can be displayed on the canvas.

Conclusions

Because we did this, we can now make the bird in our game rotate to make it look like it's falling.
You can try out this variant of the game.

//In the update method of the bird
 //change angle of bird to make it look like it is falling. -90 is straight down
    //dont make it indefinitely dependent on velocityY, or else it will keep rotating
    this.angle = Math.min(this.velocityY * 0.05, 90);

However, there are some new challenges.

Physics

This cute function is not good enough to handle rotations lol.

 //Adjusted for scaling but NOT rotation
  static checkCollision(object1, object2) {
    return (
      object1.x < object2.x + object2.width * object2.xscale &&
      object1.x + object1.width * object1.xscale > object2.x &&
      object1.y < object2.y + object2.height * object2.yscale &&
      object1.y + object1.height * object1.yscale > object2.y
    );
  }

Sprite Origins

So as of now a Game Object's origin, its x or y is the top left corner of the image. This is fine for now but it means that all rotations will happen about that point, making it look like the bird is moving in a bit wierd way... but its a bird so it looks normal lol.

If you like this and are interested in the development, do consider joining the discord server!
The Helicity.ai Team 🚀

item.title: 2023 年中国的 Web 开发人员 | A Web Developer in China, 2023
item.description:
尽管此举的动机有很多，但最主要的原因是与我妻子的家人住在一起，让我们的女儿接触到这种文化。

我的计划是继续在中国进行网络开发，但我不知道这是否可行。 正如我们许多人所知，中国严格监控该国的互联网活动，并阻止我们在该行业依赖的许多关键应用程序和服务。

在这里生活了将近一个月，我可以有把握地说，在中国作为数字游民生活确实是可能的。 然而，还有一些障碍需要克服。

伟大的防火墙

最大的障碍是谷歌、Gmail和Twitter等服务被中国的防火墙屏蔽。 我必须设置一个新的 Microsoft 电子邮件地址并在那里转发我的所有 Gmail 邮件。

过去，很容易获得VPN服务，例如Express VPN，来解决这个问题。 然而，Express VPN 在中国不再适用于我。 唯一有效的 VPN 服务是私有 VPN，但速度非常慢。

如果有人可以为中国推荐更可靠的VPN服务，请告诉我！

中国的 Web 开发工具

令我惊讶的是，Github 和 Gitlab 居然在中国工作。 我可以访问我的所有代码存储库！ 我主要使用 Next.js 进行开发并部署到 Vercel，两者都在中国防火墙后面工作。 我的主要域名注册商 Namecheap.com 也在这里工作。

您需要做的主要调整是从 Goole 切换到 Bing 作为搜索引擎，老实说，这并没有那么糟糕。

自从我来到这里以来，我已经成功开发并部署了一个完整的网站，没有遇到很多问题。 我只是希望有更好的 VPN 服务，这样我就可以更长时间地保持与 Twitter 和 Telegram 的连接。

有关中国生活的更多更新和细节，请查看我的旅游博客！ lmhotravel.com/posts/big-move-to-china lmhotravel.com/posts/big-move-to-china



原文:

In June, 2023, my family and I uprooted our lives and moved to China. Although there were many motives for this move, the primary reason was to live with my wife's family and expose our daughter to the culture.

My plan was to continue web development in China, but I had no idea if that was feasible. As many of us know, China strictly monitors the country's internet activity and blocks many crucial apps and services we rely on in this industry.

Having lived here for almost a month, I can safely say that it is indeed possible to live as a digital nomad in China. There are, however, a few hurdles to overcome.

The Great Firewall

The biggest hurdle is that services such as Google, Gmail, and Twitter are blocked by China's firewall. Telegram, Zoom, and Discord are also blocked, so communication and access to news back home is not as accessible.

Slack seems to be the only messaging app that is not blocked here, along with Microsoft Teams and Skype.

I've had to set up a new Microsoft email address and forward all of my Gmail messages there.

In the past, it was easy to get a VPN service, such as Express VPN, to solve this problem. However, Express VPN does not work for me anymore here in China. The only VPN service that works is one called Private VPN, but it is very slow.

If anyone has a more reliable VPN service that they can recommend for China, please let me know!

Web Developer Tools in China

To my surprise, Github and Gitlab actually work here in China. I have access to all of my code repos! Amazon AWS also works, which is great news if you rely of this for deployments.

I mainly develop with Next.js and deploy to Vercel, both of which work behind China's firewall. Namecheap.com, my primary domain registrar, also works here.

The main adjustment you'll need to make is switching from Goole to Bing as a search engine, which honestly isn't all that bad.

I have successfully developed and deployed a complete website since my time here, without many issues. I just wish a better VPN services was available so that I can stay connected to Twitter and Telegram for longer periods of time.

For more updates and details about life in China, check out my travel blog! lmhotravel.com/posts/big-move-to-china
item.author: changoman

item.title: AWS 助理级别认证学习指南 - 第 1 部分 | Study Guide for AWS Associate Level Certifications - Part 1
item.description:
在学习 3 个 AWS Associate 级别认证时，我开始以 Markdown 格式做笔记。

这是因为为了记住事情，我必须把它们写下来，对我正在阅读的信息进行简短的总结。 这是唯一对我有用的方法。

后来我意识到这个回顾内容除了对我考试前复习有帮助外，可能对其他人也有用，所以我决定将其发布在我的博客上。


我认为并希望它可以用作额外的学习资源、复习，甚至是对您正在接触的一些 AWS 主题的超快速介绍。

经过小幅审查后，我将在未来几周内逐步发布内容。

三项考试的内容有所不同，但有很多重叠之处，如果您正在学习AWS Certified Developer - Associate 、 AWS Certified Developer - Associate或AWS Certified SysOps Administrator -，那么总体概述在任何情况下都是有用的。准.


最后，我还会分享一个带有矩阵的表格，以便更好地了解每项考试重点关注的服务是什么。

这是今天发布的第 1 部分的内容：

在下一部分中，我将分享我关于计算、数据库、存储、开发工具等方面的笔记。

我还将发布一系列我在学习和练习时遇到的最有趣的考试样题。

原文:

While studying for the 3 AWS Associate level certifications I started taking notes in markdown format.

This is because to remember things I have to write them down, producing short summaries of the information I am reading. And this is the only method that works for me.

I later realized that this recap content, in addition to being a support to me as a refresher before the exams, might also be useful to others, so I decided to publish it on my blog.


I think and hope that it could be used as an additional study source, as a refresher, or even as a super-fast introduction to some AWS topics that you are approaching.

I will release the content gradually over the coming weeks, after a small review.

The content of the three exams is different, but there is a lot of overlap, and a general overview can be useful in any case, if you are studying for AWS Certified Developer - Associate, AWS Certified Developer - Associate or AWS Certified SysOps Administrator - Associate.


In the end, I will also share a table with a matrix to better understand what are the services on which each exam is focused.

This is the content that comes with Part 1, released today:

In the next part I will share my notes about computing, databases, storage, developer tools, and many more.

I will also release a collection of the most interesting sample exam questions that I have come across while studying and practicing.

Please let me know if you find this useful in any way or if you find errors or inaccuracies in the content.

Nirmalmahesh Subramani - Jul 4

item.title: 用于高效编程的优化代码就是您需要的工具！ | Optimized code for efficient programming IS THE TOOL YOU NEED!!
item.description:
在软件开发领域，编写高效的代码对于实现最佳性能和生产力至关重要。

直接跳转到工具！ 隆重推出EXO ，这是一款人工智能驱动的工具，旨在帮助程序员创建和改进代码以最大限度地提高效率。 凭借其一系列功能和用户友好的界面，EXO 是寻求优化工作流程的开发人员的终极解决方案。

它确定了代码可以改进的领域，例如性能优化、算法增强和代码重构。 通过实施这些建议，开发人员可以实现更快的执行时间、减少资源使用并整体提高代码质量。

灵活的计划： EXO提供不同的计划来满足不同用户的需求。 免费计划允许用户免费访问该工具的核心功能。 如需更高级的功能和增强的支持，用户可以选择 Pro 计划，该计划提供额外的好处。 轻松帐户集成：通过多个帐户集成选项，访问 EXO 变得简单。 用户可以使用 Google、GitHub 或 LinkedIn 帐户登录 EXO，从而提供无忧的入职体验。 这种无缝集成确保开发人员可以快速开始利用该工具的功能，而无需任何额外的帐户设置。

节省时间和工作流程优化：通过利用 EXO，程序员可以在开发过程中节省宝贵的时间。 这种节省时间的方式使开发人员能够提高工作效率。

直接跳转到工具！ 凭借其人工智能驱动的代码分析和优化功能，开发人员可以显着提高代码的性能和质量。 通过利用 EXO，程序员可以节省时间、增强工作流程，并最终提高开发项目的效率。



原文:
