item.title: 使用 golang 作为 Web 框架的乐趣 | Fun with golang as a web framework
item.description:
我最近正在寻找精益、干净且具有出色性能的东西来构建 SAAS 应用程序。 我只需要一个漂亮的样板，其中包含常用的东西 - 身份验证、最小后端模板、用户管理、电子邮件模板等......

使用 Jetstream 一段时间后，感觉不太对劲。 不要误会我的意思，Laravel 是一个很棒的框架，我喜欢将它用于我的大部分后端内容，但对于新项目 - 它对于我的喜好来说太臃肿了。

无框架方法

那么回到第一个点 - 我应该使用 Django 吗？ 或者节点世界中的东西？ 我以前使用过大部分这些工具，它们大多都附带电池，但问题又来了。 使用全栈框架是有代价的——你无意中固有了所有的膨胀。

然后我突然意识到：我将 Golang 用于 CLI 工具、API、抓取工具等......为什么不将其作为完整的后端框架呢？

以下是我觉得有趣的一些：

而这样的例子不胜枚举。 有很多这样的轻量级框架，它们看起来都很酷。 然而，我选择了GIN ，因为它正是我所需要的——快速且最少。

令人惊讶的是，Golang 如此之小，但拥有足够的库和工具来构建最常见的 Web 应用程序和 cli 工具。

你可能甚至不需要像 GIN 这样的框架，因为 Golang 内置的 net/http 包只需几行代码就可以生成一个漂亮的 API 服务器。 尽管如此，GIN 并没有偏离标准 Golang 太远，并且提供了默认 net/http 包中缺少的一些很好的额外功能。

到目前为止，我已经成功构建了一个样板后端：身份验证、SES 电子邮件支持、模板等...仅使用以下软件包（除了 GIN 之外）：

  • 身份验证（仅使用会话库加中间件）： github.com/alexedwards/scs/处理所有身份验证内容。 数据库处理： GORM 。 我喜欢拥有强类型结构的想法，并且 GORM 与结构配合得很好 - 如果需要，您甚至可以直接使用原始 SQL。

查看我的源代码，我将逻辑分离到标准 MVC 结构中，它看起来比大多数其他全栈框架干净得多（公平地说，Django 也非常干净）。 然而，我已经成功构建了我真正需要的大部分功能，并且不觉得自己错过了任何东西。 更酷的是，由于 Golang 是编译的，因此您可以生成一个自包含的二进制文件； 只需一个文件即可运行整个应用程序，并且无需在生产环境中安装任何依赖项。

由于 Golang 的语法非常紧凑，所以我也没有花太长时间就获得了良好的工作基础。

我还没有添加团队和计费支持（我将使用 Paddle 作为我的支付网关），所以这会很有趣，但到目前为止，我也很享受并彻底享受整个 Golang 工作流程。

我在此基础上建设的项目：
整体 - 是的，这不是一个花哨的 REACT + API 项目，而只是一个使用服务器端模板的整体，并为这些动态元素散布了一些 JavaScript。

我希望更多的人能够尝试将 Golang 作为完整的后端解决方案，而不仅仅是作为 API 或微服务层。



原文:

I was recently looking for something lean, clean and with great performance to build a SAAS app. I just needed a nice boilerplate with the usual stuff - auth,minimal backend template, user management, email templates etc...

Laravel - was my first choice, and jetstream seems like a great project with all the bells and whistles I need - so I setup a local copy and started to tinker with it.

After playing around with Jetstream for a while, it just didn't feel right. Don't get me wrong, Laravel is a great framework and I love using it for most of my backend stuff but for new projects - it's just gotten too bloated for my liking.

The no framework approach

So back to square one - should I use Django? or something in the node world? I've used most of these tools before and they mostly come with batteries included, but there again is the problem. There's a price you pay for using a full stack framework - you inadvertently inherent all the bloat.

Then it dawned upon me: I use Golang for CLI tools, API's, scrapers, etc... why not as a full backend framework?

Golang frameworks

Golang does not have a framework as complete as Laravel or Django however there are some frameworks that are pretty cool though. Here are some I found interesting:

And the list goes on. There are a ton of these lightweight frameworks and they all seem pretty cool. However, I picked GIN because it's just what I need - fast and minimal.

Surprisingly, Golang is so minimal but has just enough libraries and tools to build most common web apps and cli tools.

You probably don't even need a framework like GIN, since Golang's built-in net/http package can generate a nice API server with just a few lines of code. Nonetheless, GIN doesn't stray too far away from stock standard Golang and provides some nice extra functionality that is missing in the default net/http package.

Golang feels fun and productive

So far I've managed to build a boilerplate backend with: auth, SES email support, templating, etc... using just the following packages (in addition to GIN):

  • Authentication (just using a sessions library plus middleware): github.com/alexedwards/scs/ to handle all the auth stuff. 2FA auth was also pretty easy as well.
  • Database handling: GORM. I love the idea of having a strongly typed struct and GORM works pretty nicely with structs - you can even drop to raw SQL if needed.

Looking at my source code, I separated out logic into the standard MVC structure and it looks so much cleaner than most of the other full stack frameworks (well too be fair, Django is pretty clean too). Yet, I've managed to build most of the functionality I really need and don't feel like I'm missing anything. Even more cool, since Golang is compiled, you can generate a self contained binary; just one file to run the entire app and no dependencies needed to be installed on the production boxes.

Since Golang's syntax is so compact, didn't take me too long either to get a good base to work from.

I'm yet to add teams and billing support (I'll be using Paddle as my payment gateway) so that's going to be interesting, but so far, I'm having fun and thoroughly enjoying the whole Golang workflow too.

The projects I'm building with this base:
Scriptables - a neat self hosted tool to deploy and build laravel servers much easier.

Monolith - yeah, this is no fancy REACT + API project but merely a monolith using server side templates with some JavaScript sprinkled throughout for those dynamic elements.

Monolith's still have a place in the web arena, and Golang is proving to be a solid alternative to more mainstream full stack frameworks - not only is Golang super fast but also very productive. I hope more people will experiment with Golang as a full backend solution and not just as an API or micro-service layer.
item.author: kwnaidoo

item.title: Rewrite：一个简单而强大的 Go URL 重写器 | Rewrite: A Simple and Powerful URL Rewriter for Go
item.description:
它受到 Apache 的 mod_rewrite 模块的启发，可以用作net/http的中间件。

重写可以通过将旧的或不需要的 URL 重定向到新的 URL、强制执行主子域或简化复杂的 URL 结构来帮助您获得更好的 SEO 结果。

如何安装重写

要安装 Rewrite，您需要在系统上安装 Go。 然后，您可以使用 go get 命令下载并安装该包：

 $ go get github.com/kataras/rewrite

如何使用重写

您可以通过两种方式加载重写选项：

  1. 通过代码使用New函数和Handler方法。 这样，您就可以处理解析错误并以编程方式创建规则。
  2. 或者通过使用Load函数的 YAML 文件，该函数返回func(http.Handler) http.Handler 。 这是最常见、最简单的方法。 它会因解析错误而恐慌。

重写规则的语法为：

 REDIRECT_CODE_DIGITS PATTERN_REGEX TARGET_REPL

在哪里：

  • 例如：

    此规则使用 301 HTTP 状态代码将所有请求从相对路径/seo/*重定向到/* 。

    您还可以使用名为PrimarySubdomain的特殊选项将所有请求从根域重定向到特定子域，例如www 。

    例如：

    此选项将所有请求从example.com重定向到www.example.com 。

    例子

    让我们编写一个遵循这些重定向规则的简单应用程序：

    • 将/seo/*重定向到/*
    • 将/docs/v12*重定向到/docs
    • 将/old (.*)重定向到/
    • 将根域请求重定向到www.

    代码如下所示：

     package main import ( "net/http" "github.com/kataras/rewrite" ) func main() { mux := http.NewServeMux() // [...routes] // Load the redirect rules from a YAML file. redirects := rewrite.Load("redirects.yml") // Wrap the router with the rewrite middleware. http.ListenAndServe(":8080", redirects(mux)) }

    就是这样！ 现在您可以测试您的应用程序并查看它如何根据您的规则重定向请求。

    您可以在 GitHub 存储库的_examples文件夹中找到更多示例： github.com/kataras/rewrite/tree/main/_e ...

    结论

    它与任何net/http路由器或框架兼容，并支持正则表达式以进行灵活的匹配和替换。 它可以通过将旧的或不需要的 URL 重定向到新的 URL、强制执行主子域或简化复杂的 URL 结构来帮助您提高 SEO 性能。

    如果您想了解有关 Rewrite 的更多信息，可以查看其 GitHub 存储库： github.com/kataras/rewrite

    我希望您喜欢这篇文章并发现它很有用。 如果您有任何反馈或疑问，请随时在下面发表评论。 感谢您的阅读！ 😊 😊



原文:

Rewrite is a Go package that lets you rewrite URL paths, subdomains or hosts based on regular expressions. It is inspired by Apache's mod_rewrite module and can be used as a middleware for net/http.

Rewrite can help you achieve better SEO results by redirecting old or unwanted URLs to new ones, enforcing a primary subdomain, or simplifying complex URL structures.

How to Install Rewrite

To install Rewrite, you need to have Go installed on your system. Then, you can use the go get command to download and install the package:

$ go get github.com/kataras/rewrite

How to Use Rewrite

You can load rewrite options in two ways:

  1. Through code using the New function and Handler method. This way, you can handle parse errors and create rules programmatically.
  2. Or through a YAML file using the Load function which returns a func(http.Handler) http.Handler. This is the most common and simplest way. It panics on parse errors.

The syntax of the rewrite rules is:

REDIRECT_CODE_DIGITS PATTERN_REGEX TARGET_REPL

Where:

  • REDIRECT_CODE_DIGITS is the HTTP status code to use for the redirection, such as 301 (Moved Permanently) or 302 (Found).
  • PATTERN_REGEX is the regular expression that matches the request URL path, subdomain or host.
  • TARGET_REPL is the replacement string that contains the new URL path, subdomain or host.

For example:

This rule redirects all requests from relative path /seo/* to /* using the 301 HTTP status code.

You can also use a special option called PrimarySubdomain to redirect all requests from the root domain to a specific subdomain, such as www.

For example:

This option redirects all requests from example.com to www.example.com.

Examples

Let's write a simple application that follows these redirect rules:

  • Redirects /seo/* to /*
  • Redirects /docs/v12* to /docs
  • Redirects /old (.*) to /
  • Redirects root domain requests to www.

The code would look like this:

package main import ( "net/http" "github.com/kataras/rewrite"
) func main() { mux := http.NewServeMux() // [...routes] // Load the redirect rules from a YAML file. redirects := rewrite.Load("redirects.yml") // Wrap the router with the rewrite middleware. http.ListenAndServe(":8080", redirects(mux))
}

The YAML file would look like this:

RedirectMatch: # Redirects /seo/* to /* - 301 /seo/(.*) /$1 # Redirects /docs/v12* to /docs - 301 /docs/v12(.*) /docs # Redirects /old (.*) to / - 301 /old(.*) /
# Redirects root domain requests to www.
PrimarySubdomain: www

That's it! Now you can test your application and see how it redirects the requests according to your rules.

You can find more examples in the _examples folder of the GitHub repository: github.com/kataras/rewrite/tree/main/_e…

Conclusion

Rewrite is a handy package that lets you rewrite URLs in Go with ease. It is compatible with any net/http router or framework and supports regular expressions for flexible matching and replacing. It can help you improve your SEO performance by redirecting old or unwanted URLs to new ones, enforcing a primary subdomain, or simplifying complex URL structures.

If you want to learn more about Rewrite, you can check out its GitHub repository: github.com/kataras/rewrite

I hope you enjoyed this article and found it useful. If you have any feedback or questions, feel free to leave a comment below. Thank you for reading! 😊
item.author: kataras

item.title: 很高兴来到这里。 | Excited to be here.
item.description:
期待看到 2024 年 7 月之前的未来 12 个月会发生什么

一旦被暂停，todianedev 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，todianedev 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，todianedev 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 todianedev 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 todianedev：

使 todianedev 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 todianedev 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

 

Looking forward to seeing what happens over the next 12 months until July 2024

Dr. Artur Kirjakulov - Jul 8

Once suspended, todianedev will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, todianedev will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by todianedev will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If todianedev is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag todianedev:

Make all posts by todianedev less visible

todianedev consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging todianedev will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 通过自动化提高您的工作效率：开发人员入门指南 | Supercharge Your Productivity with Automation: A Beginner's Guide for Developers
item.description:
作为开发人员，您是否厌倦了重复性任务占用您的宝贵时间？ 您是否梦想有一个更高效的工作流程，让您能够专注于真正展示您技能的令人兴奋的挑战？ 嗯，你很幸运！ 自动化可以拯救世界，让您成为生产力超级英雄。 在本初学者指南中，我们将探讨自动化的力量以及如何利用它成为更高效的开发人员。

为什么要自动化？

说实话——重复性的任务是一种拖累。 它们会耗尽你的精力并阻碍你处理软件开发中令人兴奋和智力刺激的方面的能力。 这就是自动化的用武之地。通过自动化这些重复性任务，您可以腾出宝贵的时间和精力来专注于真正的游戏规则改变者。

自动化还为您的工作流程带来一致性和可靠性。 通过消除人为错误，您可以确保每次任务都准确高效地执行。 另外，见证您的代码为您处理平凡的任务是令人难以置信的满足！

选择正确的语言

当谈到自动化时，选择正确的语言至关重要。 您需要一种受到广泛支持、易于学习并且拥有充满活力的工具和库生态系统的语言。 在本指南中，我们将使用 Node.js 作为我们选择的语言。 为了开始您的自动化之旅，让我们深入了解一些使用 Node.js 的实际示例。 我们将重点关注两个流行的库： fs-extra和child_process 。

示例 1：复制文件

想象一下，您经常发现自己手动将文件从一个目录复制到另一个目录。 通过自动化，您可以编写一个简单的 Node.js 脚本来为您处理此任务。 就是这样：

 const fs = require('fs-extra'); async function copyFiles() { try { await fs.copy('/path/to/source', '/path/to/destination'); console.log('Files copied successfully!'); } catch (error) { console.error('Error copying files:', error); } } copyFiles();

在此示例中，我们使用fs-extra库，它在 Node.js 的内置fs模块之上提供附加功能。 在此过程中发生的任何错误都会被捕获并记录到控制台。

示例 2：运行 Shell 命令

自动化通常涉及从脚本执行 shell 命令。 使用 Node.js 中的child_process模块，您可以轻松完成此任务。 假设您需要自动运行一系列 Git 命令。 您可以这样做：

 const { exec } = require('child_process'); function runGitCommands() { const commands = [ 'git status', 'git add .', 'git commit -m "Automated commit"', 'git push', ]; commands.forEach((command) => { exec(command, (error, stdout, stderr) => { if (error) { console.error(`Error executing command: ${command}`); console.error(stderr); } else { console.log(`Command executed successfully: ${command}`); console.log(stdout); } }); }); } runGitCommands();

在此示例中，我们定义了要按顺序执行的 Git 命令数组。 您可以自定义命令以满足您的特定需求。

有效自动化的最佳实践

要成为自动化专家，请记住以下一些最佳实践：

在深入研究自动化之前，请花一些时间来计划和分解您的任务。 识别重复的部分并确定所需的结果。 这将帮助您设计更有效、更有针对性的自动化脚本。

避免编写整体脚本。 相反，请将您的自动化任务分解为模块化功能。 这提高了可重用性、可读性和可维护性。 它还允许您更轻松地测试各个组件。

不要重新发明轮子！ 利用现有的库和工具来简化您的自动化工作。 像对待任何其他代码库一样对待您的自动化脚本。 使用 Git 等版本控制系统来跟踪更改、与其他人协作，并在需要时轻松回滚。 这确保了自动化流程的可靠性和可追溯性。

记录自动化脚本对于将来的参考和协作至关重要。 包括有关如何使用脚本、需要哪些依赖项以及任何配置步骤的明确说明。 适当的文档可以帮助您和您的团队有效地理解和维护自动化工作流程。

拥抱自动化并提高您的生产力！

自动化是一种超能力，可以将您转变为高效且高效的开发人员。 通过利用 Node.js 等工具并遵循最佳实践，您可以将自己从平凡的任务中解放出来，专注于软件开发中更令人兴奋的方面。 因此，继续吧，开始以自动化的方式提高生产力。 可能性是无限的，未来的你会为此感谢你！ 快乐的自动化！ 🚀 🚀



原文:

Are you tired of repetitive tasks that eat up your valuable time as a developer? Do you dream of a more efficient workflow that allows you to focus on the exciting challenges that truly showcase your skills? Well, you're in luck! Automation is here to save the day and transform you into a productivity superhero. In this beginner's guide, we'll explore the power of automation and how you can leverage it to become a more efficient developer.

Why Automate?

Let's be honest – repetitive tasks are a drag. They drain your energy and hinder your ability to tackle the exciting and intellectually stimulating aspects of software development. That's where automation comes in. By automating those repetitive tasks, you free up precious time and mental capacity to focus on the real game-changers.

Automation also brings consistency and reliability to your workflow. By eliminating human error, you can ensure that tasks are performed accurately and efficiently every time. Plus, it's incredibly satisfying to witness your code take care of mundane tasks for you!

Choosing the Right Language

When it comes to automation, choosing the right language is crucial. You want a language that is widely supported, easy to learn, and has a vibrant ecosystem of tools and libraries. In this guide, we'll use Node.js as our language of choice. Node.js is a JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine, and it's perfect for automating tasks on both the frontend and backend of your applications.

Getting Started with Node.js Automation

To kickstart your automation journey, let's dive into some practical examples using Node.js. We'll focus on two popular libraries: fs-extra and child_process.

Example 1: Copying Files

Imagine you often find yourself manually copying files from one directory to another. With automation, you can write a simple Node.js script to handle this task for you. Here's how:

const fs = require('fs-extra'); async function copyFiles() { try { await fs.copy('/path/to/source', '/path/to/destination'); console.log('Files copied successfully!'); } catch (error) { console.error('Error copying files:', error); }
} copyFiles();

In this example, we use the fs-extra library, which provides additional functionality on top of Node.js' built-in fs module. The copy() function asynchronously copies files from the source directory to the destination directory. Any errors that occur during the process are caught and logged to the console.

Example 2: Running Shell Commands

Automation often involves executing shell commands from your scripts. With the child_process module in Node.js, you can easily accomplish this. Let's say you need to automate running a series of Git commands. Here's how you can do it:

const { exec } = require('child_process'); function runGitCommands() { const commands = [ 'git status', 'git add .', 'git commit -m "Automated commit"', 'git push', ]; commands.forEach((command) => { exec(command, (error, stdout, stderr) => { if (error) { console.error(`Error executing command: ${command}`); console.error(stderr); } else { console.log(`Command executed successfully: ${command}`); console.log(stdout); } }); });
} runGitCommands();

In this example, we define an array of Git commands to be executed sequentially. The exec() function runs each command in the shell, capturing the output and any errors that occur. You can customize the commands to fit your specific needs.

Best Practices for Effective Automation

To become a pro at automation, here are some best practices to keep in mind:

1. Plan and Break Down Tasks

Before diving into automation, take some time to plan and break down your tasks. Identify the repetitive parts and determine the desired outcome. This will help you design more effective and focused automation scripts.

2. Modularize Your Code

Avoid writing monolithic scripts. Instead, break your automation tasks into modular functions. This promotes reusability, readability, and maintainability. It also allows you to test individual components more easily.

3. Leverage Libraries and Tools

Don't reinvent the wheel! Utilize existing libraries and tools to streamline your automation efforts. The Node.js ecosystem offers a vast collection of packages that can save you time and effort.

4. Version Control Your Scripts

Treat your automation scripts like any other codebase. Use version control systems like Git to track changes, collaborate with others, and easily roll back if needed. This ensures the reliability and traceability of your automation processes.

5. Document Your Scripts

Documenting your automation scripts is crucial for future reference and collaboration. Include clear instructions on how to use the scripts, what dependencies are required, and any configuration steps. Proper documentation helps you and your team understand and maintain the automation workflow effectively.

Embrace Automation and Boost Your Productivity!

Automation is a superpower that can transform you into a highly efficient and effective developer. By leveraging tools like Node.js and following best practices, you can free yourself from mundane tasks and focus on the more exciting aspects of software development. So, go ahead and start automating your way to productivity. The possibilities are endless, and your future self will thank you for it! Happy automating! 🚀
